channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at McGill University 2027: Fully Funded Master’s Scholarships for African Leaders in Public Health, Public Policy, Nutrition, and Sustainable Agriculture
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Applications Opening Soon for One of Canada’s Most Prestigious Fully Funded Graduate Scholarship Programs for African Students The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at McGill University is now preparing to launch its 2027 application cycle, offering a transformative opportunity for talented young African leaders to pursue fully funded master’s studies at one of Canada’s most prestigious […]

The post Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at McGill University 2027: Fully Funded Master’s Scholarships for African Leaders in Public Health, Public Policy, Nutrition, and Sustainable Agriculture appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications Opening Soon for One of Canada’s Most Prestigious Fully Funded Graduate Scholarship Programs for African Students

The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at McGill University is now preparing to launch its 2027 application cycle, offering a transformative opportunity for talented young African leaders to pursue fully funded master’s studies at one of Canada’s most prestigious universities.

Hosted by McGill University in Montreal, Quebec, Canada, this highly competitive scholarship program is designed to support outstanding African students who demonstrate exceptional academic achievement, leadership potential, commitment to community service, and a strong desire to contribute to the social and economic development of Africa.

The scholarship is far more than a financial award. It is a comprehensive leadership and educational initiative that empowers scholars to become agents of change capable of transforming sectors such as public health, nutrition, public policy, food systems, environmental sustainability, and agriculture across the African continent.

Each year, the program awards approximately 35 fully funded scholarships across 13 eligible graduate programs, covering all major educational and living expenses while providing leadership development opportunities and access to one of the world’s leading academic communities.

About the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at McGill University

The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program is a global initiative that seeks to develop the next generation of ethical and transformative leaders.

At McGill University, the program specifically focuses on preparing African scholars to address critical development challenges in:

  • Sustainable agriculture
  • Food security
  • Public health
  • Nutrition
  • Environmental management
  • Water resources
  • Biotechnology
  • Public policy

The program recognizes that Africa’s future depends heavily on investing in talented young people who can drive innovation, influence policy, strengthen institutions, and improve the well-being of communities across the continent.

By combining academic excellence with leadership training, community engagement, and global networking opportunities, the scholarship creates pathways for scholars to make lasting contributions both during and after their studies.

What Makes the McGill Mastercard Foundation Scholarship Unique?

Unlike many traditional scholarships that focus only on tuition support, the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program offers a holistic support system designed to remove financial barriers and help scholars succeed academically, professionally, and personally.

The scholarship places strong emphasis on:

  • Leadership development
  • Community impact
  • Social responsibility
  • Academic excellence
  • Sustainable development
  • Long-term contribution to Africa

Successful scholars become part of a global network of Mastercard Foundation Scholars and alumni working to create positive change in their communities and countries.

Scholarship Benefits

The Mastercard Foundation Scholarship at McGill University is fully comprehensive and covers virtually all expenses associated with graduate studies in Canada.

Benefits include:

Full Tuition Coverage

The scholarship covers the complete international student tuition fees required to pursue an eligible master’s degree at McGill University.

On-Campus Housing

Scholars receive accommodation support through on-campus housing arrangements, helping ensure a smooth transition into university life.

Monthly Living Stipend

Recipients receive financial support to cover living expenses throughout their studies.

Academic Resources and Learning Support

The program provides access to academic tools, learning materials, and support services designed to maximize academic success.

Pre-Arrival and Immigration Support

Scholars receive guidance and support throughout:

  • Immigration procedures
  • Study permit preparation
  • Travel planning
  • Arrival and settlement processes

Leadership Development Opportunities

Participants gain access to:

  • Leadership training
  • Mentorship opportunities
  • Professional development activities
  • Networking events
  • Community engagement initiatives

Access to a Global Scholars Network

Students become members of an international community of Mastercard Foundation Scholars, opening doors to lifelong collaborations and opportunities.

Number of Scholarships Available

McGill University offers:

35 Scholarships Annually

These scholarships are distributed across 13 eligible graduate programs.

Competition is expected to be intense due to the program’s prestige and comprehensive funding package.

Eligible Academic Programs

The scholarship supports selected master’s programs that align with Africa’s development priorities.

Non-Thesis Programs

Eligible non-thesis programs include:

Sustainable Agriculture

  • Animal Science: Sustainable Agriculture

Environmental and Water Resources Engineering

  • Bioresource Engineering – Environmental Engineering
  • Bioresource Engineering – Integrated Water Resource Management

Food Systems and Biotechnology

  • Biotechnology
  • Food Science
  • Food Safety
  • Integrated Food & Bioprocessing

Health and Public Health

  • Human Nutrition (Project)
  • Biostatistics
  • Public Health

Public Policy

  • Public Policy

Thesis-Based Programs

Eligible thesis programs include:

  • Animal Science
  • Bioresource Engineering

Important Note for Thesis Applicants

Applicants interested in thesis-based programs must not contact professors or supervisors directly.

Instead:

  1. Apply through the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program.
  2. Wait for scholarship screening results.
  3. If selected for a fee waiver, McGill will provide instructions regarding supervisor matching.

Who Can Apply?

The scholarship targets exceptional African students who demonstrate both academic excellence and a commitment to creating positive social impact.

Applicants must:

Academic Requirements

  • Hold a bachelor’s degree or recognized equivalent.
  • Meet McGill University’s graduate admission requirements.
  • Have a degree closely related to the chosen graduate program.
  • Demonstrate strong academic performance.

Applicants with First Class Honors or equivalent qualifications are considered especially competitive.

Citizenship Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Be citizens of an African country.
  • Currently reside in Africa.

Age Requirement

Applicants must be:

  • 35 years old or younger at the time of application.

Leadership and Service

Candidates should demonstrate:

  • Community involvement
  • Leadership experience
  • Volunteerism
  • Civic engagement
  • Service initiatives

Financial Need

Applicants must clearly demonstrate that financial barriers have limited or prevented access to educational opportunities.

Commitment to Africa

Strong candidates will show:

  • A desire to contribute to their communities.
  • Commitment to Africa’s development.
  • Plans to create positive impact after graduation.

Priority Groups

The program strongly encourages applications from:

Women

Women applicants are given priority consideration.

Persons with Disabilities

Applicants living with disabilities are encouraged to apply.

Displaced Populations

Priority is given to:

  • Refugees
  • Asylum seekers
  • Internally displaced persons (IDPs)
  • Returnees

Ineligible Applicants

The following individuals are not eligible:

  • Applicants who have already completed a master’s degree.
  • Applicants currently enrolled in a master’s degree.
  • Permanent residents of Canada.
  • Permanent residents of the United States.
  • Permanent residents of the United Kingdom.
  • Permanent residents of EU countries.
  • Dual citizens with these residency privileges.

Eligible Countries

The scholarship is open to citizens and residents of every country in Africa, including:

  • Ghana
  • Nigeria
  • Kenya
  • Uganda
  • Rwanda
  • Ethiopia
  • South Africa
  • Tanzania
  • Zambia
  • Zimbabwe
  • Senegal
  • Morocco
  • Algeria
  • Egypt

And all other African Union member states.

Learn more about the Eligibility HERE

Mandatory Information Sessions

One of the most important requirements of the application process is attendance at an official information session.

Is Attendance Mandatory?

Yes.

Only eligible applicants who attend an information session will receive access to the application form.

Failure to attend an information session means you cannot apply.

Information Session Schedule

North Africa & West Africa

  • June 23, 2026
  • June 25, 2026

East Africa & Southern Africa

  • June 30, 2026
  • July 2, 2026

Central Africa

  • July 7, 2026
  • July 9, 2026

Open Session for All Regions

  • July 15, 2026

Applicants should register for the session corresponding to their region.

Technology Barriers

Applicants unable to attend because of internet or technology challenges may submit a technological barrier disclosure form.

Learn more about the Info Sessions HERE

Selection Criteria

Applications are evaluated based on:

Academic Merit

Applicants must be academically competitive for admission to McGill University.

Leadership Potential

The committee seeks transformative leaders capable of driving meaningful change.

Community Service

Applicants should demonstrate active engagement in community development initiatives.

Development Impact

Strong consideration is given to candidates whose studies can positively impact their home countries.

Overcoming Barriers

Applicants who have overcome:

  • Financial barriers
  • Social barriers
  • Personal challenges
  • Health-related challenges

may receive additional consideration.

Learn more about the Selection Criteria HERE

Detailed Application Timeline

Information Sessions

June 23 – July 15, 2026

Scholarship Applications Open

August 3, 2026
(9:00 AM EST)

Scholarship Application Deadline

August 21, 2026
(4:00 PM EST)

No late applications will be accepted.

McGill Admission Stage

September – November 2026

Selected candidates receive instructions to apply to McGill University.

University Application Deadline

December 1, 2026

Only applicants who receive fee waivers can proceed.

Admission Decisions

February – March 2027

Scholarship Selection

Mid-February – Early March 2027

Scholarship Results

Late March – Early April 2027

Understanding the Selection Process

The scholarship process involves several stages.

Stage 1: Information Session

Applicants attend a mandatory session.

Stage 2: Scholarship Application

Applicants complete the Mastercard Foundation application.

Stage 3: Pre-Screening

Applications undergo eligibility assessment.

Stage 4: Fee Waiver Invitation

Successful applicants receive fee waiver instructions.

Stage 5: McGill Admission Application

Candidates submit university applications.

Stage 6: Admission Decision

McGill evaluates academic eligibility.

Stage 7: Scholarship Selection

Final scholarship recipients are selected.

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

What Is the Purpose of This Scholarship?

The scholarship aims to support talented African leaders pursuing master’s studies in fields critical to Africa’s development, including public health, nutrition, public policy, and sustainable agriculture.

What Does the Scholarship Cover?

The scholarship covers:

  • Full tuition
  • Housing
  • Monthly stipend
  • Immigration support
  • Academic resources
  • Leadership development opportunities

How Many Scholarships Are Available?

McGill awards approximately 35 scholarships each year.

Is the Scholarship Available for Undergraduate Students?

No.

The scholarship is exclusively for master’s-level studies.

Is Attendance at an Information Session Mandatory?

Yes.

Only attendees receive access to the application form.

Can I Apply If I Don’t Meet McGill Admission Requirements?

No.

Applicants must first meet graduate admission requirements.

What Happens During Pre-Screening?

McGill evaluates:

  • Age eligibility
  • Citizenship
  • Residency
  • Academic readiness
  • Leadership profile
  • Motivation
  • Master’s degree history

How Long Does Pre-Screening Take?

Applicants can expect results within 4–6 weeks.

What Happens If I Pass Pre-Screening?

Successful candidates receive:

  • Application fee waivers
  • Instructions for applying to McGill

What Is Conditional Admission?

Conditional admission means admission is dependent on receiving the scholarship.

What Happens If I Am Admitted But Not Awarded the Scholarship?

Conditional admission will be withdrawn.

Applicants may reapply during future cycles.

Can I Appeal Scholarship Decisions?

No.

All decisions are final.

Visit HERE for more FAQs about the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at McGill University 2027.

Conclusion

The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at McGill University remains one of the most prestigious fully funded master’s scholarship opportunities available to African students. With comprehensive financial support, world-class education, leadership development opportunities, and access to a powerful global network, the program provides exceptional young African leaders with the tools needed to transform communities and drive sustainable development across the continent.

For ambitious students passionate about public health, nutrition, public policy, biotechnology, food systems, environmental sustainability, and agriculture, this scholarship represents a life-changing opportunity to study at one of Canada’s leading universities while preparing to become a catalyst for positive change in Africa.

Discover more global Scholarship and African opportunities in OFY.

The post Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at McGill University 2027: Fully Funded Master’s Scholarships for African Leaders in Public Health, Public Policy, Nutrition, and Sustainable Agriculture appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Young Changemaker Quarterly Sponsorship 2026 | Win Up to $2,000 USD for Sustainability & Social Impact Projects
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
Young people around the world are leading innovative projects that tackle some of the planet’s biggest challenges. To support these future leaders, Young Planet Leaders is offering the Young Changemaker Quarterly Sponsorship, a funding opportunity designed for students and young innovators dedicated to creating meaningful social and environmental impact. This sponsorship program recognizes young individuals […]

The post Young Changemaker Quarterly Sponsorship 2026 | Win Up to $2,000 USD for Sustainability & Social Impact Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young people around the world are leading innovative projects that tackle some of the planet’s biggest challenges. To support these future leaders, Young Planet Leaders is offering the Young Changemaker Quarterly Sponsorship, a funding opportunity designed for students and young innovators dedicated to creating meaningful social and environmental impact.

This sponsorship program recognizes young individuals who are actively working toward solutions in areas such as climate change, sustainability, environmental protection, social justice, community development, and other important global causes.

Through quarterly funding awards, selected applicants can receive financial support to expand, improve, and continue their impactful initiatives.

About the Young Changemaker Quarterly Sponsorship

The Young Changemaker Quarterly Sponsorship is an initiative by Young Planet Leaders aimed at empowering the next generation of environmental and social impact leaders.

The program seeks applicants who demonstrate:

  • Innovation and creativity
  • Commitment to sustainability and social good
  • Leadership in community or environmental projects
  • Passion for solving real-world challenges
  • Active involvement in meaningful causes

This sponsorship is specifically intended to help recipients further develop projects or organizations they are personally involved with.

The funding cannot be used for tuition fees or for-profit business activities.

Sponsorship Award Amounts

Each quarter, Young Planet Leaders offers three sponsorship awards:

  1. First Award – $2,000 USD
  2. Second Award – $1,500 USD
  3. Third Award – $1,000 USD

A total of 12 sponsorships are awarded every year.

Who Can Apply?

Applicants must meet the following eligibility criteria:

Age Requirement

  • Applicants must be under 22 years old.

Project or Contribution Requirement

Applicants should either:

  • Be working on their own project or organization related to:
    • Environmental sustainability
    • Climate action
    • Social justice
    • Community impact
    • Other meaningful causes

OR

  • Be contributing significantly to an existing project or organization focused on sustainability, environmentalism, or social impact.

What Kind of Projects Are Eligible?

The sponsorship supports a wide range of impactful initiatives, including but not limited to:

  • Climate action campaigns
  • Recycling and waste management initiatives
  • Environmental awareness programs
  • Clean energy projects
  • Community sustainability initiatives
  • Conservation programs
  • Social justice and equality projects
  • Youth advocacy movements
  • Educational outreach programs
  • Sustainable technology innovations

Applicants with creative, measurable, and community-driven ideas are strongly encouraged to apply.

How the Sponsorship Funds Can Be Used

Selected recipients are expected to use the award to strengthen or expand their impact-driven work.

Examples of acceptable uses include:

  • Purchasing project materials or equipment
  • Organizing sustainability campaigns
  • Expanding outreach activities
  • Supporting nonprofit operational costs
  • Launching educational workshops
  • Developing environmental awareness programs
  • Scaling community impact initiatives

The funds should not be used for:

  • Tuition payments
  • Personal academic expenses
  • For-profit companies or commercial business ventures

Application Deadlines

The Young Changemaker Quarterly Sponsorship accepts applications four times each year.

Submission Deadlines:

  • March 15
  • June 15
  • September 15
  • December 15

Applicants may apply multiple times per year if they are not selected initially.

However, an individual may only receive one sponsorship award per year.

Selection Process

Applications are reviewed by the Young Planet Leaders scholarship committee.

The committee evaluates candidates based on:

  • Commitment to worthwhile causes
  • Extracurricular involvement
  • Leadership and initiative
  • Academic achievements
  • Innovation and creativity
  • Potential impact of the project
  • Intended use of sponsorship funds

Successful applicants are generally notified within one month after the application deadline.

Why You Should Apply

The Young Changemaker Quarterly Sponsorship is more than just a funding opportunity. It is a platform that recognizes young leaders who are actively working to improve the world.

Benefits of applying include:

  • Financial support for impactful projects
  • Recognition for sustainability and social impact work
  • Opportunity to scale community initiatives
  • Increased visibility for youth-led projects
  • Motivation and encouragement for young innovators

For students and young leaders passionate about making a difference, this sponsorship can help turn ideas into real-world impact.

Final Thoughts

Young Planet Leaders continues to support young innovators who are committed to solving global challenges through creativity, leadership, and action.

If you are under 22 and actively contributing to environmental sustainability, social impact, or community change, this is an excellent opportunity to receive funding and recognition for your efforts.

Learn more and apply

Interested applicants are encouraged to prepare a strong application showcasing their project goals, achievements, impact, and future plans before the next submission deadline.

Click HERE to learn more and apply for the Young Changemaker Quarterly Sponsorship 2026.

Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Young Changemaker Quarterly Sponsorship 2026 | Win Up to $2,000 USD for Sustainability & Social Impact Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Over 200 Fully Funded PhD Positions at University Mohammed VI Polytechnic (UM6P) for 2026–2027: Applications Now Open
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Study in Morocco with a Fully Funded PhD Opportunity at University Mohammed VI Polytechnic Are you looking for a fully funded PhD opportunity in 2026? Applications are now open for more than 200 fully funded doctoral positions at University Mohammed VI Polytechnic (UM6P) in Morocco for the 2026–2027 academic year. This prestigious opportunity is offered […]

The post Over 200 Fully Funded PhD Positions at University Mohammed VI Polytechnic (UM6P) for 2026–2027: Applications Now Open appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Study in Morocco with a Fully Funded PhD Opportunity at University Mohammed VI Polytechnic

Are you looking for a fully funded PhD opportunity in 2026? Applications are now open for more than 200 fully funded doctoral positions at University Mohammed VI Polytechnic (UM6P) in Morocco for the 2026–2027 academic year.

This prestigious opportunity is offered through the Center for Doctoral Studies (CEDoc), UM6P’s dedicated doctoral training hub that promotes excellence, innovation, and multidisciplinary research. The university welcomes talented researchers from Morocco, Africa, and around the world who are eager to contribute to solving some of the most pressing global and regional challenges.

About the Center for Doctoral Studies (CEDoc)

The Center for Doctoral Studies (CEDoc) at University Mohammed VI Polytechnic is committed to developing the next generation of researchers, innovators, and thought leaders.

CEDoc provides doctoral candidates with:

  • High-quality academic supervision
  • Access to world-class research facilities
  • Interdisciplinary research opportunities
  • Collaboration across multiple research centers and institutes
  • Mentorship from internationally recognized experts
  • An innovation-driven research environment

The program is designed to equip PhD candidates with the skills, knowledge, and experience needed to excel in academia, industry, policy, and entrepreneurship.

Fully Funded PhD Research Areas Available

The 2026–2027 PhD call includes opportunities across numerous research units and disciplines.

Africa Business School (ABS)

Research topics include:

  • Dynamic Pricing under Uncertainty and Market Power
  • Designing Better Organizations through Behavioral Science, Collective Intelligence, and Artificial Intelligence
  • Digital Technologies and Women Entrepreneurship in Morocco
  • Climate Impacts on Household Finance
  • Artificial Intelligence and Economic Power: Utopia or Dystopia?

Africa Center of Manufacturing Excellence (ACME)

Research topics include:

  • Multi-Modal NDT Data Fusion for Structural Integrity Assessment
  • Additive Manufacturing of Structured Catalysts and Sorbents
  • Advanced Lightweight Sandwich Structures for Aerospace Applications
  • AI-Based Multi-Sensor Inspection for Composite Structures
  • Explainable Digital Twins for Predictive Maintenance

College of Agriculture and Environmental Sciences (CAES)

Research topics include:

  • Hydroclimatic Shifts in North Africa
  • Groundwater and Water Resource Management
  • Water Cycle Dynamics in Semi-Arid Regions
  • Climate Change Adaptation Research
  • Sustainable Agriculture and Environmental Systems

Additional opportunities are available across several other UM6P research institutes and centers, covering fields such as engineering, data science, artificial intelligence, sustainability, economics, manufacturing, environmental sciences, and emerging technologies.

Who Can Apply?

Applicants should hold:

  • A Master’s degree
  • An Engineering degree
  • Or an equivalent qualification relevant to the proposed research area

Candidates from all nationalities are encouraged to apply.

Application Deadline

📅 Application Deadline: July 15, 2026

Interested applicants should review the available research topics carefully before submitting their applications.

Important: Candidates may apply to a maximum of two PhD subjects.

Why Choose UM6P?

University Mohammed VI Polytechnic has emerged as one of Africa’s leading research universities, with a strong focus on innovation, sustainability, entrepreneurship, and impact-driven research.

Benefits of joining UM6P include:

  • Access to cutting-edge research infrastructure
  • Collaboration with leading international researchers
  • Strong industry and institutional partnerships
  • A vibrant international academic community
  • Opportunities to contribute to Africa’s development and global scientific advancement

How to Apply

Prospective applicants can explore the available doctoral research topics and submit their applications through the official UM6P doctoral admissions portal.

Early application is strongly encouraged due to the competitive nature of these positions.

Final Thoughts

For aspiring researchers seeking a fully funded PhD in Africa, the University Mohammed VI Polytechnic’s 2026–2027 doctoral recruitment campaign presents an exceptional opportunity. With over 200 funded positions spanning diverse disciplines, world-class research facilities, and a commitment to innovation and excellence, UM6P offers an ideal environment to pursue advanced research and make a meaningful impact.

Don’t miss your chance to join one of Africa’s fastest-growing research institutions and advance your academic and professional career.

Key Information at a Glance

  • Institution: University Mohammed VI Polytechnic (UM6P)
  • Program: Fully Funded PhD Positions
  • Number of Positions: 200+
  • Academic Year: 2026–2027
  • Location: Morocco
  • Eligible Applicants: Master’s degree, Engineering degree, or equivalent holders
  • Application Limit: Maximum of 2 research subjects
  • Deadline: July 15, 2026

For questions regarding the application process, applicants may contact: cedoc@um6p.ma

 

How to Apply

Prospective applicants can explore the available doctoral research topics and submit their applications through the official UM6P doctoral admissions portal.

Early application is strongly encouraged due to the competitive nature of these positions.

The post Over 200 Fully Funded PhD Positions at University Mohammed VI Polytechnic (UM6P) for 2026–2027: Applications Now Open appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: FirstBank Pan-African Graduate Trainee Programme 2026 for Young Graduates in Ghana and Nigeria | Launch Your Banking Career with One of Africa’s Leading Financial Institutions
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
Applications Open for the 2026 FirstBank Pan-African Graduate Trainee Programme FirstBank has officially opened applications for its highly anticipated 2026 Pan-African Graduate Trainee Programme, offering ambitious young graduates across Ghana and Nigeria an exciting opportunity to begin a rewarding career in banking and financial services. As one of Africa’s most respected and established financial institutions, […]

The post FirstBank Pan-African Graduate Trainee Programme 2026 for Young Graduates in Ghana and Nigeria | Launch Your Banking Career with One of Africa’s Leading Financial Institutions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications Open for the 2026 FirstBank Pan-African Graduate Trainee Programme

FirstBank has officially opened applications for its highly anticipated 2026 Pan-African Graduate Trainee Programme, offering ambitious young graduates across Ghana and Nigeria an exciting opportunity to begin a rewarding career in banking and financial services.

As one of Africa’s most respected and established financial institutions, FirstBank continues to invest in developing the next generation of banking professionals through its flagship Graduate Trainee Programme. This strategic initiative is specifically designed to attract, develop, and retain exceptional graduates who will contribute to the bank’s long-term growth, innovation, and transformation agenda across Africa.

The programme provides participants with an opportunity to gain practical industry experience, develop leadership competencies, build professional networks, and acquire the skills necessary to thrive in the rapidly evolving financial services sector.

About the FirstBank Pan-African Graduate Trainee Programme

The Pan-African Graduate Trainee Programme serves as a talent development platform aimed at identifying high-potential graduates and equipping them with the technical, professional, and leadership skills required to excel within the banking industry.

The programme reflects FirstBank’s commitment to nurturing young African talent and creating a pipeline of future leaders capable of driving innovation, operational excellence, and sustainable growth across the organization’s Pan-African operations.

Selected candidates will have the opportunity to work within a dynamic and professional environment while receiving structured training and exposure to various aspects of banking operations.

Why You Should Apply

The FirstBank Graduate Trainee Programme offers numerous benefits for aspiring banking professionals.

Key Benefits

  1. Professional Career Development

Participants gain access to structured training programs designed to accelerate professional growth and career advancement.

  1. Industry Experience

Successful candidates will acquire hands-on experience within one of Africa’s largest and most respected banking institutions.

  1. Exposure to Banking Operations

Trainees gain valuable insights into banking operations, customer relationship management, financial services, risk management, digital banking, and business development.

  1. Networking Opportunities

Participants will have opportunities to connect with experienced professionals, industry leaders, mentors, and fellow trainees across the organization.

  1. Leadership Development

The programme is designed to develop future leaders who can contribute to the bank’s long-term strategic objectives.

  1. Long-Term Career Opportunities

Outstanding trainees may position themselves for long-term employment opportunities and career progression within FirstBank’s Pan-African network.

Competencies and Attributes Required

FirstBank is seeking highly motivated graduates who demonstrate strong professional and personal competencies.

Applicants should possess:

Excellent Communication Skills

Strong verbal and written communication skills are essential for effective collaboration and customer engagement.

Strong Analytical Skills

Candidates should be capable of analyzing information, identifying trends, and supporting business decision-making processes.

Interpersonal and Relationship Management Skills

The ability to build productive relationships with customers, colleagues, and stakeholders is highly valued.

Self-Motivation and Results Orientation

Applicants should demonstrate initiative, accountability, and a strong drive to achieve results.

Innovation and Problem-Solving Ability

The bank seeks graduates who can think creatively, identify opportunities, and develop practical solutions to challenges.

Teamwork and Collaboration

Successful candidates should be effective team players capable of working in diverse and dynamic environments.

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for the 2026 FirstBank Pan-African Graduate Trainee Programme, applicants must meet all of the following requirements:

Age Requirement

Applicants must not be older than 28 years as of 30 June 2026.

Educational Qualification

Candidates must possess:

  • A Bachelor’s Degree with a minimum of Second Class Lower division; or
  • An HND qualification with a minimum of Upper Credit.

National Service Requirement

Applicants must have completed their National Service Scheme (NSS) on or before 30 June 2026.

What FirstBank Is Looking For

The programme is designed to identify individuals who have the potential to become future leaders within the banking sector.

The ideal candidate should demonstrate:

  • Strong academic performance
  • Leadership potential
  • Professional integrity
  • Adaptability and resilience
  • Customer-focused thinking
  • Strong work ethic
  • Passion for banking and financial services
  • Ability to learn quickly and embrace change

Programme Locations

The 2026 FirstBank Pan-African Graduate Trainee Programme is currently accepting applications from candidates in:

  • Ghana
  • Nigeria

Similar programmes are also available across other African markets within the FirstBank network.

Application Deadline

Interested applicants must submit their applications before:

28 June 2026

Applications submitted after the deadline may not be considered.

How to Apply

Eligible candidates should submit their applications through the official FirstBank careers portal.

Applicants are encouraged to apply early and ensure that all required information is accurately completed before submission.

Apply Here 

Final Thoughts

The 2026 FirstBank Pan-African Graduate Trainee Programme presents an exceptional opportunity for talented graduates seeking to launch a successful career within Africa’s banking and financial services sector.

With access to structured learning, professional mentorship, practical experience, and exposure to one of the continent’s leading financial institutions, this programme can serve as a powerful foundation for long-term career success.

If you meet the eligibility requirements and aspire to build a meaningful career in banking, now is the time to apply and take the first step toward becoming part of FirstBank’s next generation of leaders.

For more information on this internship visit here 

For more internship opportunities on OFY visit here 

The post FirstBank Pan-African Graduate Trainee Programme 2026 for Young Graduates in Ghana and Nigeria | Launch Your Banking Career with One of Africa’s Leading Financial Institutions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: University of Cape Town Project Coordinator (City Hall Fellowship) Vacancy 2026: Join the African Mayoral Leadership Initiative and Shape the Future of African Cities
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The African Centre for Cities (ACC) at the University of Cape Town (UCT) has announced an exciting opportunity for a full-time Project Coordinator (City Hall Fellowship) to join the African Mayoral Leadership Initiative (AMALI) Data Programme. This position offers professionals passionate about urban development, city governance, leadership, research, and programme management the opportunity to contribute […]

The post University of Cape Town Project Coordinator (City Hall Fellowship) Vacancy 2026: Join the African Mayoral Leadership Initiative and Shape the Future of African Cities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The African Centre for Cities (ACC) at the University of Cape Town (UCT) has announced an exciting opportunity for a full-time Project Coordinator (City Hall Fellowship) to join the African Mayoral Leadership Initiative (AMALI) Data Programme. This position offers professionals passionate about urban development, city governance, leadership, research, and programme management the opportunity to contribute to one of Africa’s most innovative city leadership initiatives.

The successful candidate will play a critical role in coordinating the City Hall Fellowship Programme while simultaneously providing administrative and operational support to the AMALI Data Programme. The position is offered on a 12-month contract from approximately June 2026 to June 2027, with the possibility of renewal.

Applications close on 26 June 2026.

About the African Centre for Cities (ACC)

Established in 2007, the African Centre for Cities is a leading urban research institute based at the University of Cape Town. The centre is housed within the School of Architecture, Planning and Geomatics in the Faculty of Engineering and the Built Environment.

ACC is recognized across Africa and internationally for its interdisciplinary approach to urban research, policy development, city leadership, and sustainable urban transformation. Drawing expertise from across the University of Cape Town, the centre addresses some of the most pressing urban challenges facing African cities today.

The institution works across several thematic areas including:

  • Urban Governance, Politics and the State
  • Urban Health
  • Urban Food Systems
  • Sustainable Infrastructure Transitions
  • Inclusive and Circular Economies
  • Culture, Technology and Society
  • Teaching and Learning the City
  • Other Urban Development Themes

About the African Mayoral Leadership Initiative (AMALI)

The African Mayoral Leadership Initiative (AMALI) is a flagship programme housed within the African Centre for Cities. The initiative focuses on supporting African mayors and city leaders in achieving their long-term development goals and creating lasting legacies within their cities.

AMALI provides mayors with ongoing technical assistance, leadership support, skills development, and strategic advisory services throughout the course of a calendar year.

A major component of the programme is the AMALI Data Programme, which supports city executives and municipal data teams in:

  • Collecting high-quality city-level data
  • Conducting data analysis
  • Using evidence-based decision-making tools
  • Improving city-scale reporting systems
  • Strengthening urban governance through data-driven leadership

The AMALI Data Programme currently consists of:

  • A Data Programme Lead
  • Four City Data Advisors

The Project Coordinator will become an integral member of this growing and impactful team.

About the City Hall Fellowship Programme

One of the most exciting aspects of this position is the opportunity to oversee the City Hall Fellowship Programme.

Through this initiative, Master’s and PhD students are embedded in participating AMALI cities across Africa for up to ten weeks. These placements provide students with practical experience while helping city leaders address key urban challenges and advance their legacy projects.

The fellowship programme creates a bridge between academic research and real-world city governance by placing talented postgraduate students directly within municipal environments.

The Project Coordinator will ensure that these placements are strategically designed, effectively managed, and deliver meaningful outcomes for both students and participating cities.

Key Responsibilities of the Project Coordinator

1. Coordinating the City Hall Fellowship Programme

The Project Coordinator will oversee all aspects of the fellowship programme.

Responsibilities include:

Establishing and Managing Advisory Structures

  • Set up an advisory group consisting of conveners from relevant Master’s programmes at UCT.
  • Coordinate regular advisory meetings.
  • Facilitate expert guidance on programme development and implementation.

Programme Alignment and Stakeholder Engagement

  • Collaborate closely with AMALI team members and external partners.
  • Ensure the fellowship programme supports the broader objectives of the AMALI initiative.
  • Strengthen partnerships with participating cities and academic institutions.

Student Recruitment and Selection

  • Design and manage recruitment processes.
  • Advertise fellowship opportunities.
  • Coordinate application reviews.
  • Establish selection committees where necessary.
  • Support transparent and competitive selection processes.

Placement Coordination

  • Engage with participating AMALI cities to identify placement opportunities.
  • Match students with appropriate city placements.
  • Connect students with suitable advisors and mentors.
  • Facilitate productive relationships between fellows and city stakeholders.

Logistics and Operational Management

  • Coordinate travel arrangements.
  • Organize accommodation and placement logistics.
  • Ensure students are effectively integrated into host city environments.
  • Manage administrative requirements throughout the fellowship period.

Monitoring and Quality Assurance

  • Support supervisors and mentors throughout student placements.
  • Monitor fellowship progress.
  • Ensure fellows produce high-quality outputs that directly support city leaders’ legacy goals.
  • Facilitate continuous improvement of programme delivery.

Documentation and Reporting

  • Maintain comprehensive programme records.
  • Document successes, lessons learned, and programme outcomes.
  • Promote the fellowship programme through communication and outreach activities.
  • Prepare reports for ACC leadership and programme funders.

Administrative Support for the AMALI Data Programme

The second major responsibility of the role involves supporting the operational effectiveness of the AMALI Data Programme.

The successful candidate will help ensure that programme milestones are achieved efficiently and on schedule.

Responsibilities include:

Programme Administration

  • Provide administrative support to the Data Programme Lead.
  • Support City Data Advisors in their activities.
  • Maintain effective programme coordination systems.
  • Assist with scheduling and programme management.

Travel Coordination

  • Organize domestic and international travel logistics.
  • Coordinate meetings and events.
  • Manage travel-related documentation and arrangements.

Office Management

  • Support the management of the AMALI Data Programme office.
  • Ensure operational systems run efficiently.
  • Assist in maintaining a productive working environment.

Why This Opportunity Matters

The Project Coordinator role offers a unique opportunity to contribute to urban transformation across Africa.

Successful candidates will:

  • Work with influential African mayors and city leaders.
  • Help shape data-driven governance across African cities.
  • Support future urban leaders through postgraduate fellowships.
  • Engage with international urban development networks.
  • Gain experience within one of Africa’s leading urban research institutions.
  • Contribute to evidence-based policy and city development initiatives.

Working Environment

The role is based within the University of Cape Town, one of Africa’s highest-ranked universities and a globally respected research institution.

The African Centre for Cities provides an intellectually stimulating and collaborative environment that combines academic excellence with practical impact.

Professionals joining the team will work at the intersection of:

  • Research
  • Urban governance
  • Public policy
  • Leadership development
  • Data-driven decision-making
  • Capacity building

Employment Equity and Diversity

The University of Cape Town is committed to excellence, diversity, inclusion, and transformation.

As a designated employer, UCT seeks to achieve its Employment Equity goals and targets and will give preference to candidates from under-represented designated groups in accordance with its Employment Equity Plan.

The university actively promotes equal opportunity and encourages applications from diverse backgrounds.

Application Deadline

Applications for the Project Coordinator (City Hall Fellowship) position close on:

26 June 2026

Interested candidates are encouraged to review the official job advert and submit their applications before the closing date.

Apply Here 

Final Thoughts

The Project Coordinator (City Hall Fellowship) vacancy at the African Centre for Cities represents an exceptional opportunity for professionals passionate about urban transformation, city leadership, programme management, and higher education engagement.

By coordinating fellowship placements across African cities and supporting the AMALI Data Programme, the successful candidate will play a direct role in strengthening city governance, advancing data-driven decision-making, and empowering the next generation of urban leaders across the continent.

For professionals seeking a meaningful career at the intersection of urban development, research, leadership, and public impact, this role offers an outstanding platform to contribute to Africa’s urban future.

For more information on this job opportunity visit here 

For more job opportunities on OFY click here 

The post University of Cape Town Project Coordinator (City Hall Fellowship) Vacancy 2026: Join the African Mayoral Leadership Initiative and Shape the Future of African Cities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: 100+ Accelerator 2026: Global Startup Program Offering Funding, Mentorship, Pilot Opportunities, and Corporate Partnerships for Sustainability Innovators
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Applications Open for the 100+ Accelerator: Scale Your Sustainability Startup with Global Industry Leaders Applications are now open for the 100+ Accelerator, one of the world’s leading sustainability-focused startup accelerator programs designed to help innovators develop, pilot, and scale transformative solutions that address some of the most pressing environmental and social challenges facing the world […]

The post 100+ Accelerator 2026: Global Startup Program Offering Funding, Mentorship, Pilot Opportunities, and Corporate Partnerships for Sustainability Innovators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications Open for the 100+ Accelerator: Scale Your Sustainability Startup with Global Industry Leaders

Applications are now open for the 100+ Accelerator, one of the world’s leading sustainability-focused startup accelerator programs designed to help innovators develop, pilot, and scale transformative solutions that address some of the most pressing environmental and social challenges facing the world today.

The 100+ Accelerator brings together groundbreaking startups, global corporations, investors, and sustainability leaders to accelerate innovation that can create meaningful impact across industries and supply chains. Through funding, mentorship, pilot opportunities, and strategic partnerships, the program empowers entrepreneurs to transform bold ideas into scalable solutions with global reach.

If your startup is solving critical sustainability challenges and is ready to test, validate, and scale its innovation with the support of major corporate partners, the 100+ Accelerator offers an exceptional opportunity to take your business to the next level.

About the 100+ Accelerator

The 100+ Accelerator was established with a simple but ambitious mission: to fuel innovation and empower startups capable of solving some of the world’s biggest sustainability challenges.

The program believes that meaningful change happens when entrepreneurs, corporations, investors, and ecosystem partners work together to develop practical solutions that create long-term environmental and social impact.

Through collaboration and innovation, the accelerator helps startups transform promising ideas into scalable businesses capable of delivering measurable results across global markets.

The program’s philosophy is built around three key principles:

1. Fueling Innovation

Supporting entrepreneurs who are developing breakthrough technologies and business models that address critical sustainability issues.

2. Empowering Startups

Providing resources, mentorship, funding opportunities, and strategic guidance needed for startup growth.

3. Scaling Impact

Helping successful innovations move beyond pilot stages to create meaningful impact at scale across industries and global supply chains.

Why the 100+ Accelerator Matters

Organizations worldwide are increasingly seeking innovative solutions to address challenges related to:

  • Climate change
  • Resource scarcity
  • Sustainable supply chains
  • Water management
  • Waste reduction
  • Circular economy development
  • Renewable energy adoption
  • Sustainable agriculture
  • Carbon emissions reduction
  • Responsible manufacturing

Startups often possess the creativity and agility necessary to solve these challenges but may lack the resources, partnerships, and market access needed to scale their innovations.

The 100+ Accelerator bridges this gap by connecting startups directly with major corporations and industry leaders capable of supporting commercialization and growth.

Program Benefits

Participating startups gain access to numerous resources designed to accelerate growth and impact.

Funding Opportunities

Selected startups receive support to develop and implement pilot projects that demonstrate the effectiveness and scalability of their solutions.

Corporate Partnerships

Participants collaborate with major multinational companies that can provide:

  • Industry expertise
  • Technical guidance
  • Market access
  • Pilot opportunities
  • Strategic partnerships

Mentorship and Coaching

Founders receive guidance from:

  • Corporate leaders
  • Industry experts
  • Investors
  • Entrepreneurs
  • Sustainability specialists

Pilot Project Development

The accelerator enables startups to test and validate their solutions in real-world operational environments.

Global Visibility

Selected startups gain exposure to:

  • Investors
  • Industry leaders
  • Policymakers
  • Media outlets
  • Sustainability networks

Access to a Global Innovation Community

Participants become part of an international ecosystem of entrepreneurs, experts, and organizations working to build a more sustainable future.

Cohort 7: Building the Future of Sustainability

The accelerator recently welcomed Cohort 7, bringing together innovative founders, corporate partners, and sustainability leaders from around the world.

The cohort represents a diverse group of startups working to create scalable solutions that improve sustainability across global supply chains and industries.

Through collaboration, mentorship, and pilot implementation, Cohort 7 participants are helping shape a more resilient and sustainable future.

Demo Day Success

The program’s impact continues to grow each year.

At Demo Day 2025, the accelerator brought together:

  • More than 470 founders
  • Corporate partners
  • Investors
  • Sustainability professionals
  • Industry experts

The event featured:

  • Startup pitches
  • Industry discussions
  • Innovation showcases
  • Partnership opportunities
  • Networking sessions

Demo Day demonstrated the power of collaboration in accelerating sustainability innovation and scaling impactful solutions.

What Types of Startups Should Apply?

The 100+ Accelerator is ideal for startups working on innovations that address sustainability challenges in areas such as:

Climate and Environmental Solutions

  • Carbon reduction technologies
  • Climate adaptation solutions
  • Renewable energy innovations
  • Environmental monitoring technologies

Circular Economy

  • Waste reduction systems
  • Recycling innovations
  • Resource efficiency technologies
  • Sustainable materials

Water Stewardship

  • Water conservation technologies
  • Water quality solutions
  • Smart water management systems

Sustainable Agriculture

  • Agricultural technology
  • Regenerative farming solutions
  • Food system innovations
  • Supply chain optimization

Sustainable Manufacturing

  • Resource-efficient production
  • Green industrial processes
  • Sustainable packaging

Supply Chain Innovation

  • Traceability systems
  • Logistics optimization
  • Sustainable sourcing technologies

Global Network and Strategic Partners

The 100+ Accelerator collaborates with a wide network of globally recognized organizations and innovation leaders.

Notable ecosystem partners and collaborators include:

  • World Economic Forum
  • Forbes
  • TED
  • EY
  • Business Insider
  • Fast Company
  • The Aspen Institute
  • One Young World
  • Female Founders
  • Highline Beta
  • Leaders Quest

These relationships provide startups with valuable visibility and opportunities to engage with influential stakeholders.

Why Startups Should Consider Applying

Participating in the 100+ Accelerator can help startups:

  1. Validate their technology in real-world environments.
  2. Gain access to corporate customers.
  3. Build strategic partnerships.
  4. Increase investor readiness.
  5. Expand market opportunities.
  6. Accelerate product development.
  7. Strengthen business models.
  8. Scale impact more rapidly.

How to Apply

Applications are currently open.

Interested startups should review the eligibility criteria and application requirements through the official 100+ Accelerator website.

Applicants are encouraged to prepare strong submissions that clearly demonstrate:

  • The sustainability challenge being addressed
  • The uniqueness of the solution
  • Potential for scalability
  • Measurable impact
  • Commercial viability
  • Alignment with sustainability goals

Apply Here 

Final Thoughts

The 100+ Accelerator continues to be one of the most influential startup programs focused on sustainability innovation worldwide. By connecting entrepreneurs with funding, mentorship, corporate partners, and pilot opportunities, the program helps transform bold ideas into scalable solutions capable of creating lasting environmental and social impact.

For startups committed to solving critical sustainability challenges and building a more resilient future, the 100+ Accelerator represents an unparalleled opportunity to accelerate growth, validate solutions, and expand impact on a global scale.

For more information on this accelerator opportunity visit here 

For more global opportunities on OFY, visit here

The post 100+ Accelerator 2026: Global Startup Program Offering Funding, Mentorship, Pilot Opportunities, and Corporate Partnerships for Sustainability Innovators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: GCSP Prize for Innovation 2026: Win CHF 10,000 for Groundbreaking Solutions to Global Security Challenges
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Applications Open for the Geneva Centre for Security Policy (GCSP) Prize for Innovation in Global Security 2026 Innovators, researchers, organizations, entrepreneurs, policymakers, and changemakers from around the world are invited to apply for the prestigious GCSP Prize for Innovation in Global Security 2026. Organized by the Geneva Centre for Security Policy (GCSP), this internationally recognized […]

The post GCSP Prize for Innovation 2026: Win CHF 10,000 for Groundbreaking Solutions to Global Security Challenges appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications Open for the Geneva Centre for Security Policy (GCSP) Prize for Innovation in Global Security 2026

Innovators, researchers, organizations, entrepreneurs, policymakers, and changemakers from around the world are invited to apply for the prestigious GCSP Prize for Innovation in Global Security 2026. Organized by the Geneva Centre for Security Policy (GCSP), this internationally recognized award celebrates bold, creative, and impactful solutions that address some of the world’s most pressing security challenges.

Since its launch in 2015, the GCSP Prize for Innovation has become a leading platform for recognizing outstanding achievements in global security. The award honors individuals and organizations that develop innovative approaches, technologies, research, programs, and initiatives capable of improving peace, stability, resilience, and security worldwide.

The competition is open to applicants from all sectors and disciplines, reflecting the growing need for interdisciplinary collaboration in addressing modern security threats.

About the Geneva Centre for Security Policy (GCSP)

The Geneva Centre for Security Policy is an international foundation dedicated to advancing peace, security, and international cooperation. Through its educational programs, policy dialogue initiatives, research activities, and innovation-focused projects, the GCSP serves a global community of professionals, institutions, and leaders working to build a more secure world.

The Prize for Innovation in Global Security is part of the GCSP’s Creativity and Innovation Initiative and is managed by the organization’s Geopolitics and Global Futures Department.

What Is the GCSP Prize for Innovation?

The GCSP Prize for Innovation recognizes exceptional achievements that contribute innovative solutions to contemporary and emerging security challenges.

The award seeks to identify transformative ideas and initiatives that have the potential to make a significant impact on global peace and security. Winning projects often demonstrate originality, scalability, effectiveness, and practical relevance to today’s complex security environment.

The competition welcomes a broad range of innovations, including:

  • New technologies addressing security challenges
  • Research publications advancing security knowledge
  • Policy innovations and governance solutions
  • Conflict prevention initiatives
  • Peacebuilding programs
  • Cybersecurity innovations
  • Humanitarian security interventions
  • Community resilience projects
  • Climate-security solutions
  • Artificial intelligence applications for security
  • Educational initiatives promoting peace and stability
  • Institutional reforms and innovative organizational approaches

Prize Benefits

Successful applicants will receive international recognition and valuable opportunities to showcase their work to a global audience.

First Place Winner

The first-place laureate will receive:

  • CHF 10,000 cash prize
  • Certificate of Excellence
  • International recognition from a leading global security institution
  • Increased visibility among policymakers, researchers, practitioners, and security experts

Second and Third Place Winners

Recipients will receive:

  • Official Certificates of Recognition
  • International exposure and professional recognition

Honourable Mentions

Two additional projects will receive:

  • Honourable Mention Awards
  • Recognition for outstanding contributions to global security innovation

Why You Should Apply

Participating in the GCSP Prize for Innovation offers numerous advantages:

1. Global Visibility

The award provides an international platform to showcase innovative work before experts, practitioners, governments, international organizations, and global decision-makers.

2. Financial Support

The CHF 10,000 cash award can help scale impactful projects and support future innovation efforts.

3. Professional Recognition

Winning or being shortlisted by the Geneva Centre for Security Policy can significantly strengthen professional credibility and organizational reputation.

4. Networking Opportunities

Participants gain exposure to a global community of innovators, researchers, policymakers, and security professionals.

5. Impact Amplification

The competition helps promising solutions reach wider audiences and potential partners capable of expanding their impact.

Who Should Apply?

The GCSP Prize welcomes applications from a diverse range of actors including:

  • Academic researchers
  • Universities and research institutions
  • Non-governmental organizations
  • Civil society organizations
  • Technology companies
  • Startups
  • Government agencies
  • International organizations
  • Independent innovators
  • Peacebuilders
  • Humanitarian actors
  • Security practitioners
  • Policy experts
  • Social entrepreneurs

Applicants from all sectors are encouraged to submit innovative work that demonstrates measurable contributions to global security.

Areas of Interest

The competition is particularly relevant for projects focused on:

  • International security
  • Peace and conflict resolution
  • Strategic foresight
  • Cybersecurity
  • Artificial intelligence and security
  • Geopolitics
  • Human security
  • Climate and security
  • Countering violent extremism
  • Crisis management
  • Humanitarian innovation
  • Defense innovation
  • Governance and resilience
  • Emerging technologies
  • Global futures and strategic competition

Application Deadline

Applicants must submit their applications no later than:

23 September 2026 at 23:59 UTC+2

Late applications may not be considered.

Winners Announcement

The winners of the 2026 GCSP Prize for Innovation will be announced during Fall 2026.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should review the official rules and regulations and submit their applications through the Geneva Centre for Security Policy application portal.

Applicants are strongly encouraged to begin preparing their submissions early and ensure that all required information is completed before the deadline.

Apply Here 

Final Thoughts

The GCSP Prize for Innovation 2026 represents a remarkable opportunity for individuals and organizations working to transform the future of global security. Whether through technological innovation, groundbreaking research, policy development, or practical peacebuilding initiatives, this award recognizes those whose work is shaping safer and more resilient societies.

With a CHF 10,000 cash prize, international recognition, and the opportunity to engage with a global network of security professionals, the GCSP Prize for Innovation remains one of the most prestigious opportunities for innovators committed to advancing peace and security worldwide.

If your work is creating meaningful solutions to today’s most pressing security challenges, this is an opportunity worth pursuing.

For more information on this competition visit here 

For more global opportunities on OFY, visit here 

The post GCSP Prize for Innovation 2026: Win CHF 10,000 for Groundbreaking Solutions to Global Security Challenges appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: OECD Middle East and Africa Division Internship 2026: Launch Your International Policy Career with the OECD
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
Aspiring policy professionals, researchers, economists, and international development enthusiasts now have an exciting opportunity to gain hands-on experience at one of the world’s leading international organizations. The Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) Middle East and Africa Division is currently seeking an intern to support the work of the division beginning in September 2026. […]

The post OECD Middle East and Africa Division Internship 2026: Launch Your International Policy Career with the OECD appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Aspiring policy professionals, researchers, economists, and international development enthusiasts now have an exciting opportunity to gain hands-on experience at one of the world’s leading international organizations.

The Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) Middle East and Africa Division is currently seeking an intern to support the work of the division beginning in September 2026. This internship offers a unique opportunity to contribute to impactful policy initiatives, regional economic development programmes, and international cooperation efforts focused on the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region.

Candidates with an interest in economic development, investment, competitiveness, entrepreneurship, and public policy are strongly encouraged to apply. While not a mandatory requirement, working knowledge of Arabic is considered a significant advantage.

Interested applicants are invited to submit their CV directly to:

sara.mouhoud@oecd.org

About the OECD

The Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) is an international organization that works with governments, policymakers, and stakeholders worldwide to develop evidence-based policies that promote prosperity, equality, opportunity, and well-being.

The OECD serves as a global platform for policy dialogue, research, data analysis, and international cooperation, helping countries address economic, social, and environmental challenges.

Working within the OECD provides unparalleled exposure to international policymaking, research, and development initiatives that influence governments and institutions across the globe.

About the Middle East and Africa Division

The OECD Middle East and Africa Division plays a crucial role in strengthening partnerships and supporting policy reforms across countries in the Middle East and North Africa region.

The division works closely with governments, policymakers, private sector stakeholders, development partners, and international institutions to promote:

  • Economic growth
  • Sustainable development
  • Investment promotion
  • Private sector development
  • Entrepreneurship
  • Job creation
  • Economic competitiveness
  • Youth empowerment
  • Women’s economic participation

Through research, policy dialogue, technical assistance, and capacity-building initiatives, the division contributes to the economic transformation and resilience of countries throughout the region.

The MENA-OECD Competitiveness Programme

One of the flagship initiatives supported by the OECD Middle East and Africa Division is the MENA-OECD Competitiveness Programme.

Established in 2005 at the request of governments across the Middle East and North Africa, the programme serves as a strategic partnership between OECD and MENA economies.

For over two decades, the programme has facilitated:

  • Knowledge exchange
  • Policy dialogue
  • Capacity building
  • Sharing of international best practices
  • Investment promotion initiatives
  • Entrepreneurship development
  • Economic reform support

As the programme celebrated its 20th anniversary in 2025, it continues to serve as a critical platform for fostering inclusive, sustainable, and competitive economies throughout the region.

Objectives of the MENA-OECD Competitiveness Programme

The programme operates under the mandate of the 2021 Ministerial Declaration and focuses on supporting reforms that help countries:

1. Mobilize Investment

The programme supports efforts to:

  • Improve investment climates
  • Attract foreign direct investment
  • Strengthen investor confidence
  • Enhance regulatory frameworks

2. Strengthen Private Sector Development

Activities focus on:

  • Business environment reforms
  • SME development
  • Competitiveness enhancement
  • Innovation promotion

3. Foster Entrepreneurship

The programme supports:

  • Startup ecosystems
  • Entrepreneurial capacity building
  • Innovation-driven growth
  • Youth entrepreneurship initiatives

4. Promote Inclusive Growth

A major emphasis is placed on creating opportunities for:

  • Young people
  • Women
  • Emerging entrepreneurs
  • Underserved communities

Countries Participating in the Programme

The MENA-OECD Competitiveness Programme currently engages 18 economies:

  1. Algeria
  2. Bahrain
  3. Djibouti
  4. Egypt
  5. Iraq
  6. Jordan
  7. Kuwait
  8. Lebanon
  9. Libya
  10. Mauritania
  11. Morocco
  12. Oman
  13. Palestinian Authority
  14. Qatar
  15. Saudi Arabia
  16. Tunisia
  17. United Arab Emirates
  18. Yemen

These countries collaborate with OECD member states to exchange expertise and develop policies that support economic transformation and sustainable development.

How the Programme Operates

The programme functions through a structured governance framework that includes:

High-Level Ministerial Meetings

Senior government officials and policymakers gather to:

  • Set strategic priorities
  • Discuss regional challenges
  • Share policy experiences
  • Coordinate reform efforts

Annual Steering Group Meetings

The Steering Group monitors:

  • Programme implementation
  • Progress toward objectives
  • Future priorities
  • Regional cooperation initiatives

Regional Policy Dialogues

The programme organizes policy discussions across multiple thematic work streams to encourage knowledge sharing and collaborative problem-solving.

Country-Specific Projects

Tailored support is provided through:

  • Dedicated Country Programmes
  • Bilateral initiatives
  • Technical assistance projects
  • Capacity-building activities

Notable examples include:

  • OECD Country Programme with Egypt
  • Bilateral initiatives with Morocco
  • Bilateral initiatives with Saudi Arabia

What the OECD Internship Could Involve

Although specific responsibilities have not been publicly detailed, interns in OECD divisions commonly contribute to:

Research and Analysis

  • Conducting background research
  • Reviewing policy developments
  • Analyzing economic and social data
  • Supporting report preparation

Programme Support

  • Assisting with project coordination
  • Supporting event organization
  • Preparing briefing materials
  • Contributing to programme activities

Stakeholder Engagement

  • Supporting communication with partners
  • Assisting with outreach activities
  • Coordinating meetings and workshops

Knowledge Management

  • Compiling research findings
  • Drafting summaries and reports
  • Supporting publication development

Benefits of the Internship

An internship with the OECD offers numerous professional advantages.

International Experience

Gain firsthand experience working within a globally respected international organization.

Policy Exposure

Develop an understanding of:

  • Economic policymaking
  • International cooperation
  • Development strategies
  • Regional reform initiatives

Professional Networking

Interact with:

  • OECD experts
  • Government officials
  • International development practitioners
  • Researchers
  • Policy specialists

Career Development

The experience can strengthen future career prospects in:

  • International organizations
  • Government ministries
  • Development agencies
  • Think tanks
  • Research institutions
  • Consulting firms
  • Multilateral organizations

Why Arabic Language Skills Are Valuable

The Middle East and Africa Division works extensively with stakeholders across the MENA region.

As a result, candidates with working knowledge of Arabic may benefit from:

  • Enhanced communication with regional partners
  • Greater understanding of regional contexts
  • Ability to engage with Arabic-language materials
  • Increased effectiveness in programme activities

However, Arabic remains an asset rather than a mandatory requirement.

How to Apply

Click here for more and to apply

Why You Should Apply

An internship with the OECD Middle East and Africa Division provides a rare opportunity to contribute to meaningful policy work that supports economic transformation, entrepreneurship, investment, and sustainable development across the MENA region.

For students and young professionals seeking a career in international affairs, economics, public policy, or development, this internship offers valuable exposure to global policymaking processes and the chance to work alongside leading experts shaping the future of economic cooperation and reform.

Candidates interested in launching an international policy career are encouraged to apply as early as possible.

Click here for more global internship opportunities

The post OECD Middle East and Africa Division Internship 2026: Launch Your International Policy Career with the OECD appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: European Youth Energy Forum (EYEF) 2026 in Nice, France: Applications Open for Students, Young Professionals, and Researchers Driving the Energy Transition
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The European Youth Energy Forum (EYEF) 2026 is now accepting applications for its highly anticipated fifth edition, offering an exceptional opportunity for students, young professionals, researchers, and emerging leaders to engage directly in shaping the future of Europe’s energy transition. Scheduled to take place from 26–29 October 2026 in Nice, France, the forum will bring […]

The post European Youth Energy Forum (EYEF) 2026 in Nice, France: Applications Open for Students, Young Professionals, and Researchers Driving the Energy Transition appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The European Youth Energy Forum (EYEF) 2026 is now accepting applications for its highly anticipated fifth edition, offering an exceptional opportunity for students, young professionals, researchers, and emerging leaders to engage directly in shaping the future of Europe’s energy transition.

Scheduled to take place from 26–29 October 2026 in Nice, France, the forum will bring together some of the brightest young minds from across Europe and the Mediterranean region to exchange ideas, collaborate on solutions, strengthen professional networks, and contribute to discussions that will influence the future of sustainable energy systems.

Organized by the European Youth Energy Network (EYEN), EYEF has become one of Europe’s leading youth platforms dedicated to empowering the next generation of energy professionals and ensuring that youth perspectives are represented in the global energy dialogue.

Applications are open from 11 May to 21 June 2026, and selected participants will embark on a multi-month journey that combines virtual learning, collaboration, and networking activities before culminating in the in-person forum in Nice.

About the European Youth Energy Forum (EYEF)

The European Youth Energy Forum is more than a conference.

It is a youth-driven platform designed to connect ambitious young people who are passionate about energy, climate action, sustainability, innovation, and policy development.

Since its inception, EYEF has provided a unique space where participants can:

  • Discuss pressing energy challenges
  • Explore innovative solutions
  • Learn from industry experts
  • Collaborate across disciplines
  • Build international partnerships
  • Develop leadership skills
  • Contribute to Europe’s energy transition agenda

The forum recognizes that young people are not simply future stakeholders in the energy sector—they are already playing critical roles in shaping sustainable energy systems, influencing climate action policies, and advancing innovation across industries.

EYEF 2026 Theme: From Skills to Impact

The 2026 edition of the forum is built around the theme:

“From Skills to Impact: Empowering Young Professionals to Implement the Energy Transition”

This theme reflects a growing recognition that achieving energy transition goals requires more than technical knowledge alone.

It requires:

  • Leadership
  • Collaboration
  • Innovation
  • Practical implementation skills
  • Cross-sector partnerships
  • Youth engagement

The forum seeks to equip participants with the tools, networks, and experiences needed to translate knowledge into real-world impact.

Why EYEF 2026 Matters

Europe is undergoing one of the most significant energy transformations in history.

Countries across the continent are investing heavily in:

  • Renewable energy
  • Green hydrogen
  • Energy efficiency
  • Smart grids
  • Sustainable mobility
  • Climate resilience
  • Decarbonization strategies

At the same time, governments, industries, and institutions increasingly recognize the importance of involving young people in decision-making processes.

EYEF serves as a bridge between youth talent and the institutions driving Europe’s energy future.

The forum provides participants with opportunities to:

  • Engage with industry experts
  • Learn from policymakers
  • Connect with researchers
  • Collaborate with peers
  • Develop innovative solutions

A Unique International Experience

One of the defining features of EYEF is its international and interdisciplinary nature.

The forum welcomes participants from diverse academic and professional backgrounds, creating a rich environment for knowledge sharing and collaboration.

Participants typically represent fields such as:

  • Renewable energy
  • Engineering
  • Environmental sciences
  • Climate policy
  • Sustainable development
  • Economics
  • Energy law
  • Innovation and entrepreneurship
  • Research and academia

This diversity allows participants to explore energy challenges from multiple perspectives and develop more comprehensive solutions.

Building on Collaboration with IRENA

The European Youth Energy Forum continues its collaboration with the International Renewable Energy Agency (IRENA) Youth Forum.

This partnership strengthens connections between regional and global youth energy initiatives and provides participants with greater exposure to international energy discussions.

The collaboration contributes to:

  • Global knowledge exchange
  • International networking opportunities
  • Exposure to renewable energy developments
  • Youth participation in global energy governance

What Selected Participants Can Expect

Participants selected for EYEF 2026 will participate in a comprehensive programme that extends beyond the in-person event.

Online Learning Journey

Before arriving in Nice, participants will engage in an online programme running from July to October 2026.

This phase is designed to:

  • Introduce key energy transition topics
  • Facilitate networking
  • Prepare participants for the forum
  • Encourage collaborative learning

In-Person Forum in Nice

The in-person event will take place from 26–29 October 2026.

During the forum, participants can expect:

  • Interactive workshops
  • Expert discussions
  • Collaborative sessions
  • Networking events
  • Team-based activities
  • Youth-led initiatives
  • Professional development opportunities

Long-Term Network Access

Participants become part of the wider European Youth Energy Network community, providing opportunities for future collaboration and engagement.

Who Should Apply?

EYEF is designed for motivated individuals who are passionate about creating a sustainable energy future.

Ideal applicants include:

Students

University students studying:

  • Energy
  • Engineering
  • Environmental sciences
  • Climate studies
  • Public policy
  • Economics
  • Sustainability

Young Professionals

Early-career professionals working in:

  • Energy companies
  • Utilities
  • Government agencies
  • NGOs
  • Climate organizations
  • Research institutions

Researchers

Researchers exploring topics related to:

  • Renewable energy
  • Climate change
